title: Understand: weather
description: Check your understanding of weather in this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two properties define an air mass?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which two properties define an air mass?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which two properties define an air mass?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**How do air masses move in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They flow from regions of low pressure to regions of high pressure.
They flow from regions of high pressure to regions of low pressure.
They flow between regions of equal pressure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do air masses move in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: from areas of high pressure to areas of low pressure
from areas of low pressure to areas of high pressure

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do air masses move in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: from low-pressure regions to high-pressure regions
from high-pressure regions to low-pressure regions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option to complete the sentence.**

>A warm front occurs when $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a warm air mass moves into a cold air mass
a cold air mass moves into a warm air mass
two air masses meet and stop moving

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does a warm front form?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A cold air mass moves into a warm air mass.
A warm air mass moves into a cold air mass.
Two air masses meet and stop moving.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does a cold front form?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A warm air mass moves into a cold air mass.
A cold air mass moves into a warm air mass.
Two air masses meet and stop moving.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What type of weather do cold fronts usually bring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What type of weather do cold fronts usually bring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What type of weather is associated with cold fronts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
